_doc: Registry of known settlement culture traits. The layout generator reacts to traits it recognises and ignores unknown ones — so new traits can be added freely by agents before handler code exists. When adding a new trait, include a one-line description so other agents know what it means.
builds-underground: Some structures are subterranean pits, dugouts, or tunnel entrances
no-central-plaza: Culture doesn't gather centrally; distributed or multi-node layout
water-integrated: Canals, stilts, floating platforms, or amphibious access between buildings
living-architecture: Buildings are grown organically (coral, resin, fungus), not constructed
nomadic-core: Central structure is temporary (tent, yurt, pavilion); periphery may be permanent
vertical-stacking: Builds upward — towers, elevated walkways, cliff-face nesting, bridges
ruin-incorporators: Actively builds into or onto pre-existing ruins and salvaged structures
symmetry-obsessed: Layouts are mirrored along one or two axes; strong geometric order
chaos-builders: Intentionally irregular; no two buildings alike, organic sprawl
communal-walls: Buildings share walls or form connected complexes rather than standing apart
fortified-perimeter: Settlement has a defined defensive boundary — wall, ditch, palisade, or hedge
elevated-construction: Buildings raised on stilts, platforms, or natural rock above ground level
subterranean-preference: Most living space is below ground; surface structures are minimal
secreted-structures: Buildings formed from biological secretion (resin, silk, chitin) not quarried material
crystal-resonant: Structures incorporate or are built around crystal formations; layout follows crystal veins
mechanical-integration: Settlement includes functional machinery, gears, pipes, or automata as infrastructure
song-mapped: Layout follows acoustic or navigational-chant logic rather than visual geometry
wind-oriented: Buildings and streets aligned to prevailing winds; windcatchers, sails, open corridors
fire-centred: Central communal fire or forge is the social and spatial anchor, not a plaza
seasonal-shift: Some structures are temporary or move with seasons; layout changes over the year
bridges-between: Buildings connected by elevated walkways or bridges; ground level is secondary
carved-not-built: Structures carved from existing rock, ice, or terrain rather than assembled
flooded-intentionally: Parts of the settlement are deliberately underwater or waterlogged by design
light-sensitive: Layout avoids or seeks sunlight deliberately — shade structures, reflectors, blackout walls
scent-marked: Territory and building function communicated by scent, not signs — affects spacing and airflow
